This is all without premium and I’d like to at-least be able to choose what songs I listen to and skip as much as I want. text:
Given enough time - all software decays. Spotify is a prime example of this phenomenon  especially in the age of AI. When you're building what's basically a utility/tool for a lot of people at this point - stability must be a foundational pillar in my opinion. But the quality bar has dropped drastically in the last two quarters of 2026. 

Home screen ui issues, random app crashes, terrible apple tv app experience. It's all over the place. Please add relevant bad customer experience metrics like app abandoned, session abandon, app force close. I bet these are up significantly. And please consider taking some basic goals to bring them down over next few quarters. Shipping features for the sake of shipping features ends badly - I speak from experience as a veteran iOS dev. If you wanna ship more - do it in a new standalone app, why ruin the main app?
title: Buggy mess - quality and stability of the app feel off a cliff in 2026

ALSO this part drives me so mad the most: You can't rewind the song or go back to your favorite part in the song. If you want to listen to a song again, you have to click it, which will most likely just give you an ad. If you listen to a song just because of one part, don't do it on Spotify. It's a really great music app IF you have premium, otherwise, YouTube is infinitely better.

text: I do not like that if I choose to play a song on regular Spotify (without premium), they won’t play the song I chose and that I get limited time to listen to what I actually want to listen to. Like after a while, they just won’t let me pick a song. Also, if I want to listen to a full album, Spotify will play similar songs INSTEAD of what I actually want to listen to. Like bruh, you can do that after I’m done listening to what I’m actually using your app for. And I can’t turn off shuffle?? If I’m getting 5 ads every 2 songs then I better be able to listen to what I want. I understand why ads because they have to make money somehow, but they really are doing too much with all the other stuff they have going on. But Spotify premium is fire which is why I still have it. They do need to fix regular Spotify tho. I like how Spotify is set up and I like my playlists.  But I hate that free music is so hard to access. And you may be asking, why not just use YouTube? And I would except I can’t turn off my screen and it takes up more battery. So anyway, Spotify, GET YOUR ACT TOGETHER!! 🪼🐠🐬💃🪩😒

text:
I really like the app overall, but the shuffle feature is very inconsistent. I have a huge Liked Songs library, yet it feels like I hear the same tracks over and over. 

I’ll start playing a random song, and the queue is filled with songs I listened to just a day or two ago, often in nearly the exact same order. Even if I choose a different random song to start with, it might change a few songs in the queue, but it quickly goes right back to the same group of tracks. 

A true shuffle should do a much better job of pulling from my entire library.

text:
Spotify is honestly the best music app out there. The absolute best part is how it completely gets my music taste. My Discover Weekly and Daily Mixes always have the best recommendations, and I’ve found so many cool new artists through them. It also has literally every song you could ever think of, plus podcasts, and it’s super easy to use on my phone or computer. 

The only thing that really annoys me though is the explicit content filter. It is way too strict and blocks so many songs that don’t even have curse words in them. It's super frustrating when a good song is totally censored or skipped just because of the theme of the song. I really wish they would fix that so it actually lets you listen to the real versions of songs.

author: Hilux n70

text: I dislike the lack of functionality in the macbook app, compared to the iphone app.  Also why on earth is there not an "add to play next" type button/option, as opposed to merely a "add to queue" option? It is so annoying that when you already have a long queue, you can only add an item to the end of the queue, and the you have to manually move that item all the way up to the beginning of the long queue just because you actually want hear that item *next*.  Furthermore, I want to be able to create *multiple* queues. That is because everyday I spend time and effort creating a long queue of podcasts I want to listen to for that day, BUT I don't want to listen to then all in one sitting -- often I want to listen to various music throughout the day too. So please let me create a podcast queue and *seperate* music queue (and, for the avoidance of doubt, that is NOT the same as creating multiple playlists because I don't want to keep these queues as playlists once the day is finished).
title: Add a “Play Next” button

author: Jaxon finch

text: I’ve rated Spotify before and previously gave it 5 stars. However, there is one thing I would like to mention that lowers its rating. The algorithm that recommends music to you is not great. I don’t know if it’s a cost issue or just that the algorithm isn’t great but it will recommend me really random songs from genres that I have never listened to in my life. I’m not against broadening my music taste however it becomes frustrating when you want to listen to a specific music genre and all it recommends are genres you have no care for. All that being said it is a genuinely good app and despite popular opinion, I personally love their Spotify wrapped. I recommend people to download this app because it genuinely is worth it. If you do not have Spotify premium the adds can be bothersome however there are other options like a family plan that are cheaper than Spotify premium that get rid of the ads. Overall it’s a great app and a worthwhile investment!!

author: explodingstar25

text:
Spotify used to be great, but now I’m finding the app incredibly confusing. There are so many tabs, and some of them seem to appear more than once. I also have to scroll endlessly through album icons that I never selected. I’m talking pages and pages of them.

The user experience is a mess. And what the hell is the new icon anyway?

When I search for something, I get endless results that have nothing to do with what I’m looking for. Classical music is especially a nightmare. If a piece has more than one movement, I have to click on every single track just to hear it or see the full title before I can choose the one I want.

The only reason I’m still using Spotify is because I’ve built playlists over the last 10 years. Ironically, those playlists are so hard to find that I can spend the first three minutes in the app just trying to locate them.

I’d much rather see my own playlist icons than random miscellaneous junk I don’t care about. It would be so nice if the app were simpler, cleaner, and actually useful.

Spotify, we can do better.
title: Spotify: we need to talk

author: Yumtruth

text:
While I’m a long-term fan and heavy user of the platform, persistent dissatisfaction has accompanied the experience on 3 main points.   Firstly the interface continues in a dated idea of how modern UI can be.  Second, the home page and the suggestions of what I might like have always offered me so much of what I have never and won’t ever listen to.  Lastly that singular option of a ridiculous voice and manner of the ai DJ.  It’s absurd and jarring.  
That said, other aspects of the algorithm are great.  Discover Weekly has given me so much over the years, so for now it remains the home of my countless playlists.

author: Jdjlo

text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

J’utilise Spotify depuis longtemps et c’est de loin la meilleure application de musique que j’ai essayée. L’interface est simple, moderne et très facile à utiliser. J’adore pouvoir découvrir de nouvelles chansons grâce aux playlists personnalisées et aux recommandations qui correspondent vraiment à mes goûts musicaux.

La qualité du son est excellente et il y a un immense choix de musique, de balados et de contenus pour tous les styles. J’aime aussi le fait de pouvoir créer mes propres playlists et les partager avec mes amis. L’application fonctionne très bien et je l’utilise tous les jours, que ce soit à la maison, à l’école, en voiture ou pendant mes activités.

Spotify rend l’écoute de musique agréable et pratique. Les mises à jour apportent régulièrement de nouvelles fonctionnalités intéressantes et l’expérience s’améliore constamment. Je recommande cette application à tous ceux qui aiment la musique. Merci à toute l’équipe Spotify pour cette excellente application !

text: I love and hate Spotify. First : I have it only because certain songs aren’t available on iTunes due to copyright. 2. I cannot customise Home Screen or even recommendations. I already pay subscription so why I have to deal with what your AI or you think I will like ?! Let those enjoying features for those who aren’t willing to pay ! The new icon : what the hell have you been thinking about?! It’s horrible ! Please add more option to customise  not just Home Screen but the whole experience.! We already pay. Leave the version that isn’t possible to customise to the people that aren’t willing to pay ! It’s easy. Also one podcast isn’t available in my region ok get it ( there isn’t better podcast app that Apple one let’s be honest here ! ) and yet I cannot delete it from my screen and it’s still on my list ! Again we pay monthly fee and it’s not a low amount ! What are your developers doing ?! What are you paying them for ?! Are you 1. lazy to add the customise options 2. Unable to do so ?! Do two versions of Spotify one for paying customers where they will be able to even choose better icon because this one is horrible and customise Home Screen etc. and second one for the ones that aren’t willing to pay. It makes me feel like you don’t appreciate paying customer.

I love the ease of the app and the breadth of music I can listen to anywhere. 
I’m not appreciating the use of AI and grateful I can not engage it mainly. 

And I do recognize that you are on the boycott list due to your links to Israel and arms supplying. 
And while it is harder and harder to separate ourselves from the implicit support of war, we can’t directly save these people being bombed and killed for the sake of capitalism and power. We can name and ask the companies we support to disengage from these machines of war. 
What say you Spotify?

author: My not so good review

text:
I thought the music video / podcast update would be so good, I was so wrong. First of all the music videos take ages to load and are usually low quality. Second of all the podcast videos get recommended on your homepage with clickbait thumbnails worse than YouTube, and if you like listening to a few specific true time podcasts all they’ll recommend is horror videos and even innapropriate titled videos with creepy thumbnails.
I go onto Spotify to listen to music at night and I’m met with horrible thumbnails, I don’t know how it takes over my page considering I click the NOT INTERESTED button everytime, clearly doesn’t seem to work. Even podcasts I’ve listened to once keep getting recommended on repeat and I didn’t like them that’s why I listened once, I don’t follow these podcasts and press not interested and they STILL get recommended. Just because I like some true crime girlies doesn’t mean I want horrific recommendations. I pressed follow on podcasts I am interested in and not interested on podcasts I don’t like and would honestly block if I could. What’s the point in these buttons if they don’t work? Honestly it’s ruined my entire Spotify experience not to mention it takes up more energy and storage on my phone over an update that literally IS SO ANNOYING. I want to play music and listen to podcasts. This update is literally taking away from my music experience. AND IM PAYING FOR IT? No thanks I hope Spotify can fix these issues soon because I was a big fan of the app.
title: Video update is the WORST

text: I’m genuinely appalled, this is the only free music app I can listen to an even then I can’t listen to it. You’re a music app designed to play songs that I chose? You only have 10 skips a day and I’m not sure how many songs you can play but you can only play a small amount within a day, but it’s SUPPOSED to reset once a day and you have skips and a choice of songs to play, apparently not anymore. And no, I can’t jut get premium. It’s expensive, I don’t have the money to get 3 meals a day in this economy and you think I’m going to go out of my way for a music app? Yeah I wish but no, anyway in a nutshell.. this app could be good but its bad features suck way more than its good features perform well.

text:
Spotify has completely changed the way I experience music. The sound quality is excellent, the recommendations feel incredibly personalized, and I’m always discovering new artists and playlists that match my vibe perfectly. The interface is clean and easy to use, whether I’m on my phone, laptop, or car.
I especially love the curated playlists and features like Discover Weekly — they consistently introduce me to songs I end up playing on repeat. Streaming is smooth, downloads work flawlessly, and the premium experience is absolutely worth it.

text: I’ve paid for Spotify premium for like seven years and it was fine, until I discovered that there was no way for me to filter out and avoid all of the terrible AI music that is clogging up the platform. So I cancelled my premium for this reason, and I’m infuriated to learn that you can only play albums in the intended order on your mobile app if you have premium which I think is a really ridiculous feature. I accept that if I don’t have premium I have to listen to ads, but to not be able to play an album in order is infuriating. I will be finding another music service. Which is a shame because I liked Spotify until I couldn’t avoid AI music and couldn’t play my albums in order. Ai and predatory subscription models ruin everything.

text:
I’ve never had any complaints about Spotify, until the last few weeks. 

It is now regularly stopping playing music. It says, “We’re having trouble playing this song. Try going online…”

It happens even when I’m online.

If I kill the app and restart it, the same song that was failing will pick up exactly where it left off. I can also start playing a new playlist.

Having this happen when I’m driving means I can’t play music until I can safely pull over and restart the app. It doesn’t matter if I’m using CarPlay or not, it happens either way. 

It happens to everybody in my family, as well. There’s no explanation that makes sense, except for a new bug being introduced that’s impacting playback.

text:
Spotify plays podcasts again right after finishing them. 
There is no option of blocking content/creators (even temporarily)and podcasts/content I have no interest anymore appear in the auto-feed which cannot be influenced.
No private mode in the app - when kids play their music (which might not be available in the Spotify kids version) it affects recommendations and playlists created by Spotify.

The issues above have been addressed online for years with Spotify not reacting or suggesting a complete account reset.

This is poor behalf and maintenance on Spotify‘s behalf and a switch to another provider might be the only alternative.
title: Software issues not adressed for years

author: Layney Willson

text:
STEPS: 
When searching inside a playlist (pull down from top) the initial search bar shows one line down from the header. 
BUT On tap it jumps one line above…. 😡
Often with a delay…leading to a second tap to initiate search. 
ISSUE: Results in accidentally selecting the first search result in the list 😞
Ruins your currently playing playlist selecting the wrong track on the new playlist. Ruining playing song for audience. 

Solutions:
- Search shows in the top line on pull down (same line as back)
- Or search stays on the second line (same place) the whole time. 
- Or start search result list one line down so one can’t select the first track by accident. 
- fix delay. Delay has been improved (worse on older devices) but still not good when searching your playlists to queue tracks for an audience.

text: Spotify used to be great and then recently it’s started to be so ridiculously slow, I’ve tried turning off extra features like Canvas and Smart Shuffle, removed downloads, cleared cache, dropped audio and visual quality, checked stuff with my own phone and it’s still SO slow! Checked it across multiple devices too and the same thing! This started a few months ago and seems to be a problem with every person I’ve talked to who uses Spotify as well. Just so many unnecessary features that slow down the app with no way to turn them off: concerts, merch, DJ, song DNA etc. At what point do you add so many features that it ruins the basic function of the app? Why can I load all of these extra features but can’t see the tracklist of an album or play a song? Just ridiculous.
title: So slow?

text:
Goodbye Spotify, I’m cancelling my subscription. I received an email today informing me of a $2 price increase per month. Well that’s the final nail in the coffin. I’m not paying extra for a service that isn’t fit for purpose. Your podcast queue feature has been broken for some time. Queues will suddenly disappear and random episodes are added to the end of any queue I create. So instead of the queue finishing, it continues with episodes I don’t want. Also, episodes listened to are no longer deleting automatically despite having that option selected. 
The measly amount of audiobook hours isn’t worth paying premium for either. Goodbye Spotify, It’s sad to see a great service in decline.
title: Fail to address podcast issues

I listen to music on Spotify every single day, absolutely love the platform, and will never tire of it!
Also love the long free trials of Spotify Premium. They are incredible.
The only thing I find hard to get my head around is how expensive Spotify Premium is when you come to pay for it.
It’s nearly $20 per month, and I think it could be a bit cheaper and more affordable for those who love the idea of listening to unlimited, uninterrupted music but aren’t able to pull that much money out of their pockets monthly, because Spotify is so good that everyone should have a taste of all it has to offer. 
Genuinely so grateful for it.

text:
Brawl Stars is more than just a mobile game—it is a complete, polished, and deeply engaging experience that represents what mobile gaming can be at its very best. From its fast-paced action and unique characters to its constant updates and strong sense of community, Brawl Stars has proven itself to be a standout title in the crowded world of mobile games. Developed by Supercell, a company already known for successful games like Clash of Clans and Clash Royale, Brawl Stars builds upon that legacy and pushes it even further. The game deserves a full five-star rating not only because it is fun, but because it consistently delivers quality, fairness, creativity, and long-term enjoyment.
At its core, Brawl Stars is a multiplayer action game that focuses on short, intense battles. Matches are quick, usually lasting only a few minutes, which makes the game perfect for both short play sessions and longer gaming marathons. This design choice is one of the game’s greatest strengths. Players never feel like they are wasting time, and every match feels meaningful. Whether you win or lose, you gain experience, learn new strategies, and improve your skills. This constant sense of progression keeps players motivated and engaged.
One of the most impressive aspects of Brawl Stars is its wide and diverse roster of characters, known as brawlers. Each brawler has a unique personality, attack style, super ability, and role on the battlefield. Some brawlers specialize in close-range combat, while others excel at long-range attacks, area control, healing, or support. This variety allows players to find characters that match their preferred playstyle. It also encourages experimentation, as trying new brawlers can completely change how the game feels. Unlocking new brawlers is exciting and rewarding, and mastering them provides a strong sense of accomplishment.
The depth of gameplay in Brawl Stars is another reason it stands out. While the controls are simple and easy to learn, the strategies involved can be surprisingly complex. Positioning, timing, map awareness, teamwork, and decision-making all play important roles in determining the outcome of a match. Skilled players learn when to attack, when to retreat, and how to work together with teammates to control objectives. This balance between accessibility and depth makes Brawl Stars appealing to players of all skill levels, from beginners to competitive veterans.
Game modes are a major highlight of Brawl Stars. The game offers a wide range of modes, each with its own objectives and strategies. Gem Grab focuses on collecting and holding gems, requiring teamwork and coordination. Showdown, available in solo and duo formats, is a survival-based mode that emphasizes individual skill and smart decision-making. Brawl Ball combines action with sports-inspired gameplay, creating fast and chaotic matches that are incredibly fun. Other modes like Heist, Hot Zone, Knockout, and special limited-time events ensure that there is always something new and exciting to play. The rotating nature of these modes keeps the game fresh and prevents it from becoming repetitive.
The maps in Brawl Stars are thoughtfully designed and play a crucial role in gameplay. Each map has its own layout, featuring walls, bushes, open areas, and choke points that influence how matches unfold. Different brawlers perform better on different maps, which adds another layer of strategy. Learning map layouts and understanding which brawlers work best in each situation is part of what makes the game so engaging. The frequent rotation of maps ensures that players must constantly adapt and refine their strategies.
Visually, Brawl Stars is vibrant, colorful, and full of personality. The art style is cartoonish yet polished, making the game appealing to players of all ages. Each brawler is visually distinct, with expressive animations and creative designs that reflect their abilities and personalities. The environments are equally well-designed, with bright colors and clear visual cues that make gameplay easy to follow even during intense moments. The visual clarity is especially important in a fast-paced game like Brawl Stars, and Supercell has done an excellent job in this area.
Sound design and music also contribute significantly to the overall experience. Each brawler has unique sound effects and voice lines that add charm and character. The background music fits the game’s energetic tone and changes depending on events and seasons, helping to keep the experience fresh. These audio elements may seem small, but they play an important role in making the game feel alive and immersive.
One of the most important reasons Brawl Stars deserves a five-star rating is its fair and well-balanced progression system. Many mobile games rely heavily on pay-to-win mechanics, but Brawl Stars avoids this pitfall. While players can spend money to speed up progression or unlock cosmetic items, skill and strategy remain the most important factors in winning matches. Free-to-play players can still enjoy the full game experience, unlock brawlers, and compete at high levels without feeling disadvantaged. This fairness builds trust between the developers and the player base.
The Brawl Pass system is a great example of thoughtful game design. It provides a clear progression path with rewards that feel meaningful and satisfying. Both free and paid tracks offer valuable items, ensuring that all players benefit from playing regularly. Completing quests and challenges feels rewarding without being overly demanding, striking a healthy balance between effort and reward. This system encourages consistent play while respecting players’ time.
Customization is another strong aspect of Brawl Stars. Players can personalize their experience with skins, pins, sprays, and other cosmetic items. These customizations allow players to express themselves and show off their favorite brawlers without affecting gameplay balance. Many skins are creative,
title: Brawl Stars: A Five-Star Masterpiece of Mobile Gaming

text:
“Enjoy your 30 minutes of ad free music”… ads then continue to play after every song, sometimes not even playing the last 5 seconds of the song before disrupting it with an ad. 

Spotify acts as if it specifically doesn’t want their users to have an enjoyable listening experience, As you can not pick the specific song you want to listen to, even if you play a 10 hour playlist that has that same song repeated for that 10 hour duration, they detect you’re attempting to listen to a song YOU want to listen to, so they play a song of a complete different genre by a complete different artist.

It’s clear this is a money hungry app that doesn’t have much consideration at all for users not willing/can’t afford a monthly/weekly subscription.

If this is what you’re after then use this app. 

Also, If you are excited for your  “Spotify wrapped” just know it’s likely to be inaccurate..

author: Stark 0999

text:
Music > Following is excluding new releases from artists I follow now.  

Has become painfully slow recently. Takes up to 20 seconds before music loads and starts playing if you haven’t been using the app or it’s been paused too long. 

Spotify needs to update their features and transparency with AI music. Spotify has become flooded with AI generated music and fake bands and does not flag or notify users, or give them an option to block generated music. 

The smart shuffle feature can be a great way to find new songs but functions strangely by playing a predetermined selection of songs depending on which song you start with in your playlist. So it doesn’t actually shuffle randomly. It also seems to only shuffle from certain sections depending on where you start it in a playlist if you have a lot of songs in the playlist like your Liked Songs playlist. Sponsored recommendations are awful and borderline paid for ads from artists trying to get noticed. Regardless of your tastes or genres they will get pushed in your face based on your country and will not be to your tastes at all. I get better artist recommendations on Spotify in Instagram ads which is a joke. The AI DJ is…yeah. Just no. There is no intelligence there at all. Just lazily pushing genres and top artists based on your location again instead of finding you new music based on your actual tastes and listening history. Gave up trying to train it and repeatedly hiding the same artists and songs I don’t like.
title: Modern Features Required

Please fix the performance issues because the app experience right now is terrible.
